description: A YouTube export (Studio CSV + Takeout) builds records with stats, tags, retention, and Studio-CSV revenue; most-watched is derived from retention.
assertions: records carry tags and view stats with provenance
retention is present and most_watched is derived
revenue appears only from the Studio CSV
the importer proposes; nothing is saved automatically

description: An Instagram export builds records but retention and most-watched are null-flagged, never estimated.
library-complete fills a missing transcript on-device and joins it to the YouTube retention curve so each most-watched moment carries the spoken words.
assertions: a matched media file gets a local STT transcript with provenance
each most-watched peak carries the words spoken at that timestamp
audio and transcript stay on the machine

description: With no STT backend installed, the library completes metadata-only and transcripts carry the run_local_stt gap, never a fabricated transcript.
assertions: transcripts are flagged run_local_stt with the per-OS install command
the metadata library is still built

description: Analysis of the completed library cites video_keys and null-flags unavailable data.
assertions: top tags and retention insights cite video_keys
non-YouTube retention is listed under retention_unavailable
